records.ety: [OE. fulfillen, fulfullen, AS. fulfyllan; ful full + fyllan to fill. See Full, a., and Fill, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fulfilled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Fulfilling.]

defs: To fill up; to make full or complete.
quotes: Suffer thou that the children be fulfilled first, for it is not good to take the bread of children and give to hounds.

defs: To accomplish or carry into effect, as an intention, promise, or prophecy, a desire, prayer, or requirement, etc.; to complete by performance; to answer the requisitions of; to bring to pass, as a purpose or design; to effectuate.
quotes: He will, fulfill the desire of them fear him.
Here Nature seems fulfilled in all her ends.
Servants must their masters' minds fulfill.
